questions.prompt: Complete: "Every morning, Yassine ___ up at six o'clock."
questions.explanation: "Every morning" describes a daily routine, so we use the simple present. Yassine = he, so the verb takes -s: he gets up. "Get" forgets the -s, "got" is the past, and "getting" cannot stand alone.

questions.prompt: Which adverb of frequency means 0% (not one time)? "Mariem is a vegetarian, so she ___ eats meat."
questions.explanation: A vegetarian does not eat meat at all, so the adverb is "never" (0%). "Always" means every time, "usually" means most times, and "sometimes" means now and then.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Last night, I ___ my English lesson for two hours."
questions.explanation: "Last night" is a finished past time, so we use the simple past. "Study" ends in consonant + y, so the y changes to i before -ed: studied. "Studyed" keeps the y (wrong spelling), and "study/studies" are present forms.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We ___ a great football match on TV yesterday."
questions.explanation: "Yesterday" is a finished past time. "See" is irregular, and its simple past is "saw": we saw a match. "Seen" is the past participle (used with have), and "see/seed" are not correct past forms.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: "___ you finish your homework last night?"
questions.explanation: "Last night" shows a finished past time, so a past question uses "Did" + subject + base verb: Did you finish? "Do/Does" are present, and "Were" is the verb "to be", which does not fit before "finish".

questions.prompt: Complete: "My father ___ to Tunis every Monday for work."
questions.explanation: "Every Monday" shows a habit, so we use the simple present. My father = he, so the verb takes -s: travels. "Travelled" is the past, "travel" forgets the -s, and "is travel" is not a correct form.
